figures.caption: Figure 1: Test 1 - 1D unsteady Burgers. Convergence histories. Losses for PINN, objectives for adjoint, forcing errors, and the wall-clock times are shown. Horizontal axes indicate quasi-Newton iteration.
Figure 2: Test 1 - 1D unsteady Burgers. Recovered forcing field (top) and terminalstate field (bottom). εu is calculated based on the terminal state uNt .

Figure 3: Test 2 - 2D Darcy. Convergence histories with noisy observations. Losses for PINN, objectives for adjoint, log-permeability errors, and the wall-clock times are shown. Horizontal axes indicate quasi-Newton iteration.

Figure 4: Test 2 - 2D Darcy. Recovered log-permeability frecovered (top) and pointwise error frecovered− f⋆ (bottom), shown for the adjoint, PINN, and EKI estimators. The 9×9 observation grid is overlaid on the reference panel (green dots).
Figure 5: Test 2 - 2D Darcy. Recovered state urecovered (top) and pointwise error urecovered − u⋆ (bottom).

Figure 6: Test 3 - 3D Allen–Cahn. Convergence histories. Losses for PINN, the objective for adjoint, reaction-term errors, and the wall-clock times are shown. Horizontal axes indicate quasi-Newton iteration.
Figure 7: Test 3 - 3D Allen–Cahn. Recovered reaction term frecovered (left) and pointwise error frecovered − f⋆ (right) on the physical range u ∈ [−1, 1].

Figure 8: Test 3 - 3D Allen–Cahn. Slice (z = 0.336) of the recovered terminal state uNt recovered (top) and pointwise error relative to u⋆Nt (bottom). εu is calculated based on the full terminal state uNt .

Figure 9: Test 4 - 2D unsteady Navier–Stokes cylinder wake. Left: PINN composite loss (top) and trainable viscosity (bottom), Adam→SSBroyden transition marked. Right: adjoint reduced objective (top) and recovered viscosity (bottom), for both the baseline adjoint and the PINN-seeded restart. Wall-clock times are annotated in the legends.

Figure 10: Test 4 - 2D unsteady Navier–Stokes cylinder wake. Velocity-magnitude snapshots at t ∈ {0, 2.5, 5}. Left: FEM reference with the 16 wake probe locations (green crosses). Middle: inverse-PINN field at the converged viscosity. Right: pointwise absolute error. The probes constrain only a small wake window, yet drive the recovered viscosity to within 5.2× 10−3 relative error.
